subject: DevOps
title: Repeatability and Consistency
description: A micro-lesson that makes Repeatability and Consistency usable.

hook.text: Dev has 2 subnets. Staging has 3. Production has 4. Nobody remembers why they're different. They shouldn't be.

buildup.text: Manual setup leads to drift. Each environment evolves independently, accumulating unique differences over time.

discovery.text: IaC uses the same code for every environment. Variables customize the size, but the structure stays identical.

twist.text: Consistency eliminates 'works in staging but fails in production' surprises. Same code, same result, guaranteed.

climax.text: Spinning up a new environment takes minutes, not weeks. Run the code and get an exact replica every time.

punchline.text: Same code, same infrastructure. Repeatability kills configuration drift.

quiz.question: How does IaC prevent configuration drift between environments?
options: The same code defines every environment with variable customization
It locks environments from any changes after creation
